rationale: Review 1’s decisive placement finding is confirmed by the diff: the public API is generic bialgebra/Hopf-algebra convolution machinery, with no actual algebraic-group/functor-of-points infrastructure, so moving/splitting it would yield a better-organized PR. Review 2 identifies a real narrower namespace/privacy issue for `antipode_convMul_id`, but acting only on that would leave the broader placement problem essentially intact.
